spec_match.rationale: Side-by-side report shapes per cookie-state combination implements the spec rule correctly.

interactive.rationale: Cookie-state selects per side drive both report panels, plus a privacy-chip indicator and a leakage explanation that updates per combination.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises toggle pub/ad cookie states with side-by-side reports and cross-site join explanation — page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: All four 3PC combinations are reachable but the cross-app/web variant (also relevant per spec) is absent — only the report-diff-viewer's debug-key-privacy sibling models that branch.

references.rationale: Only siblings linked in see-also (need to verify the read-ahead — but based on the first 100 lines, no chromestatus link is visible; treating as partial pending full read. If absent, this is a major fail.

design_system.rationale: Lines 91-93 set .privacy-chip.safe/.exposed/.partial with the SAME color for background AND color (e.g. background:var(--accent-emerald);color:var(--accent-emerald)). That's invisible text — fails WCAG AA absolutely (1:1 ratio). This is exactly the rule #6 / rule #5 bug class CLAUDE.md warns against.

openQuestions.title: Fix the invisible privacy-chip text
openQuestions.detail: The .privacy-chip.safe / .exposed / .partial rules set background and color to the SAME variable — chips render as solid blocks of color with no readable label. Pick a contrast pair (background:--accent-emerald + color:--bg-ivory, or vice versa) per state. CLAUDE.md rule #6 explicitly calls out this trap.

openQuestions.title: Verify chromestatus link in references
openQuestions.detail: Confirm and (if missing) add the https://chromestatus.com/feature/6257907243679744 link to the see-also section. Rule #3 requires it on every concept page.

openQuestions.title: Add cross-app/web context selector
openQuestions.detail: The sibling debug-key-privacy demo has a 'cross-app/web' branch. Add it here too so the diff-viewer covers the full spec rule (3 axes: publisher cookie, advertiser cookie, cross-app context) rather than just the 2x2 cookie matrix.

summary: Solid side-by-side report comparison undermined by a visible-text bug — the privacy-chip styles render labels in the same colour as their background. This is the exact rule-#6 invisible-button trap CLAUDE.md warns against and needs fixing before this page is presentable.
